response.justification: The candidate is an experienced (10+ years) and versatile software engineer, but their depth is in web/backend stacks (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the C++/STL core this role demands. The only professional C++ exposure is roughly six months at National Instruments around 2014–2015, plus a university thesis project. There is no evidence of message brokers, MQTT, IoT/industrial protocols, secure development under IEC 62443, or UML-driven architecture in an automation context. The mismatch on the central technical requirement (modern, hands-on C++) makes this a poor fit despite the strong general engineering background.
strengths: 10+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Demonstrated low-latency, multi-threaded, event-driven systems work (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services)
Strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD) and proven mentoring/teaching ability
concerns: Minimal and dated C++/STL experience (~6 months ~10 years ago); primary stack is TS/Node/DLang
No evidence of MQTT, message brokers, IoT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No exposure to Secure Development Lifecycle or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity standards
